item.title: cmd_backlog_check returns 0 when readiness gates fail
item.intent: backlog check must return non-zero when gates fail — callers and scripts must be able to rely on exit status to detect unready items.
item.description: cmd_backlog_check() calls check_readiness_gates() and logs warnings on failure, but does not return 1. The function exits 0 regardless of gate outcome. Any script using 'backlog check $ID && do_something' will proceed even when gates fail, undermining the purpose of the check command. Fix: add 'return 1' in the else branch after printing gate warnings.
steps: After the 'echo -e "$CHECK_GATES_WARNINGS"' line in the else branch, add 'return 1'
Verify that 'backlog check <ready-ID>' exits 0 and 'backlog check <unready-ID>' exits non-zero
acceptanceCriteria: backlog check <ID> exits non-zero when any readiness gate fails
backlog check <ID> exits 0 when all readiness gates pass
Exit status matches the log output — if warnings are printed, exit is non-zero
